for i = 1, 10 do
  assert(a[i](i * 10) == i % 3 and a[i]() == i * 10)

print'+'


-- test for correctly closing upvalues in tail calls of vararg functions
local function t ()
  local function c(a,b) assert(a=="test" and b=="OK") end
  local function v(f, ...) c("test", f() ~= 1 and "FAILED" or "OK") end
  local x = 1
  return v(function() return x end)
end
t()


-- test for debug manipulation of upvalues
local debug = require'debug'

local foo1, foo2, foo3
do
  local a , b, c = 3, 5, 7
  foo1 = function () return a+b end;
  foo2 = function () return b+a end;
  do
    local a = 10
    foo3 = function () return a+b end;
  end
end

assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 1))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 2))
assert(not debug.upvalueid(foo1, 3))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 1) == debug.upvalueid(foo2, 2))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 2) == debug.upvalueid(foo2, 1))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo3, 1))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 1) ~= debug.upvalueid(foo3, 1))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 2) == debug.upvalueid(foo3, 2))

assert(debug.upvalueid(string.gmatch("x", "x"), 1) ~= nil)

tool_use_result.stdout:

print'+'


-- test for correctly closing upvalues in tail calls of vararg functions
local function t ()
  local function c(a,b) assert(a=="test" and b=="OK") end
  local function v(f, ...) c("test", f() ~= 1 and "FAILED" or "OK") end
  local x = 1
  return v(function() return x end)
end
t()


-- test for debug manipulation of upvalues
local debug = require'debug'

local foo1, foo2, foo3
do
  local a , b, c = 3, 5, 7
  foo1 = function () return a+b end;
  foo2 = function () return b+a end;
  do
    local a = 10
    foo3 = function () return a+b end;
  end
end

assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 1))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 2))
assert(not debug.upvalueid(foo1, 3))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 1) == debug.upvalueid(foo2, 2))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 2) == debug.upvalueid(foo2, 1))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo3, 1))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 1) ~= debug.upvalueid(foo3, 1))
assert(debug.upvalueid(foo1, 2) == debug.upvalueid(foo3, 2))

assert(debug.upvalueid(string.gmatch("x", "x"), 1) ~= nil)

delta.text: Build is green
. No temporary `eprintln` was added (didn't need any — the b

ug was located by reading the source).

## Report

**Root cause:** `lua_upva
luejoin` in `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:2128` was a stub (it called
`get_upval_ref_idx` for both ends and discarded the indices), so `debug.upva
luejoin(foo3, 2, foo2, 2)` at `closure.lua:264` was a no-op — `foo3()
` still returned `10 + 5` (its own original `b=5`) instead of `10 + 3` (foo2's `a=3` after

the join), tripping the assert at line 266.

The real obstacle was that `LuaL
Closure.upvals: Vec<GcRef<UpVal>>` had no interior mutability, so replacing one slot in a

shared `Rc<LuaLClosure>` was impossible.

**Fix:**
- Changed `LuaLClosure.upvals` to `Vec
<RefCell<GcRef<UpVal>>>` (`lua-types/src/closure.rs`) and added `upval(i)` / `set_upval(i, v)

` helpers.
- Updated read sites in `lua-vm/src/{api.rs,state.rs,
debug.rs}` and the construction sites in `lua-cli/src/main.rs` + `state.rs` to wrap/

unwrap the `RefCell`.
- Implemented `upvalue_join` to actually do `lcl1.set_upval(

idx1, lcl2.upval(idx2))`, sharing the GcRef<UpVal>.
- Bonus: the
global-table-rebuild workaround at `
api.rs:1665` (which previously rebuilt the whole closure due to miss

ing mutability) is now an in-place `set_upval(0, …)`.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean.
I did not re-run the test (per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test).

Build is green. No temporary `eprintln` was added (didn't need any — the bug was located by reading the source).

## Report

**Root cause:** `lua_upvaluejoin` in `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:2128` was a stub (it called `get_upval_ref_idx` for both ends and discarded the indices), so `debug.upvaluejoin(foo3, 2, foo2, 2)` at `closure.lua:264` was a no-op — `foo3()` still returned `10 + 5` (its own original `b=5`) instead of `10 + 3` (foo2's `a=3` after the join), tripping the assert at line 266.

The real obstacle was that `LuaLClosure.upvals: Vec<GcRef<UpVal>>` had no interior mutability, so replacing one slot in a shared `Rc<LuaLClosure>` was impossible.

**Fix:**
- Changed `LuaLClosure.upvals` to `Vec<RefCell<GcRef<UpVal>>>` (`lua-types/src/closure.rs`) and added `upval(i)` / `set_upval(i, v)` helpers.
- Updated read sites in `lua-vm/src/{api.rs,state.rs,debug.rs}` and the construction sites in `lua-cli/src/main.rs` + `state.rs` to wrap/unwrap the `RefCell`.
- Implemented `upvalue_join` to actually do `lcl1.set_upval(idx1, lcl2.upval(idx2))`, sharing the GcRef<UpVal>.
- Bonus: the global-table-rebuild workaround at `api.rs:1665` (which previously rebuilt the whole closure due to missing mutability) is now an in-place `set_upval(0, …)`.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean. I did not re-run the test (per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test).

result:
Build is green. No temporary `eprintln` was added (didn't need any — the bug was located by reading the source).

## Report

**Root cause:** `lua_upvaluejoin` in `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:2128` was a stub (it called `get_upval_ref_idx` for both ends and discarded the indices), so `debug.upvaluejoin(foo3, 2, foo2, 2)` at `closure.lua:264` was a no-op — `foo3()` still returned `10 + 5` (its own original `b=5`) instead of `10 + 3` (foo2's `a=3` after the join), tripping the assert at line 266.

The real obstacle was that `LuaLClosure.upvals: Vec<GcRef<UpVal>>` had no interior mutability, so replacing one slot in a shared `Rc<LuaLClosure>` was impossible.

**Fix:**
- Changed `LuaLClosure.upvals` to `Vec<RefCell<GcRef<UpVal>>>` (`lua-types/src/closure.rs`) and added `upval(i)` / `set_upval(i, v)` helpers.
- Updated read sites in `lua-vm/src/{api.rs,state.rs,debug.rs}` and the construction sites in `lua-cli/src/main.rs` + `state.rs` to wrap/unwrap the `RefCell`.
- Implemented `upvalue_join` to actually do `lcl1.set_upval(idx1, lcl2.upval(idx2))`, sharing the GcRef<UpVal>.
- Bonus: the global-table-rebuild workaround at `api.rs:1665` (which previously rebuilt the whole closure due to missing mutability) is now an in-place `set_upval(0, …)`.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean. I did not re-run the test (per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test).
